title: MapSpec
description: The contract between a builder's natural-language directive and a rendered alchemy map. Claude fills this from a directive; the generator renders it deterministically.

directive.description: The original natural-language request this spec was compiled from (provenance).

scope.description: What gets included. Empty/omitted fields are unconstrained.

region_bbox.description: [west, south, east, north] in degrees. Defines the viewport + spatial clip.

regions.description: Named regions from the data (e.g. 'Germany', 'Bohemia') as an alternative to a bbox.

themes.description: Concept slugs from the database; an event matches if it carries any of these.

persons.description: Person slugs to spotlight or restrict to.

review_status.description: Only include events at/above these provenance tiers.

style.description: Initial runtime theme. Legacy aliases: parchment=illuminated, sepia-toner=copperplate, muted-vector/modern-osm=atlas.

historical_boundaries_year.description: If set, embed period political boundaries for the nearest cached year (data/boundaries/, from historical-basemaps; run scripts/fetch_boundaries.py to populate). Rendered as a toggleable 'Borders' layer with an automatic provenance caveat.

layers.description: Which visual layers to draw, in order.

narrative.description: Editorial framing rendered as an intro panel. provenance_note is REQUIRED: the compiler refuses to build a map that does not say where its claims come from (override with --allow-unsourced for drafts).
